title: Do not auto-advance carousels without user control
summary: AI-generated marketing pages frequently use auto-advancing hero carousels where slides rotate before users finish reading, reducing content comprehension and user control.
instruction: If using a carousel, do not auto-advance slides. Let users navigate via explicit arrows, dots, or swipe gestures. If auto-advance is required, provide a visible pause button and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
rationale: Auto-advancing carousels advance before users finish reading the current slide, reducing content comprehension and user control. The rotating element can also trigger motion sensitivity. Manual navigation ensures users control their reading pace.

violations: A hero section with a 3-slide carousel auto-rotating every 4 seconds with no pause control.
A testimonial carousel auto-advancing every 3 seconds before users can finish reading.
controls: A hero section with manual arrows and dots only, no auto-advance.
A testimonial section showing all testimonials in a scrollable grid rather than a carousel.
borderline: A rotating banner for urgent system alerts where auto-advance is justified by critical information volume, provided a pause button exists.

exceptions.description: Kiosk or digital signage displays with no user input, video content that is inherently temporal, and photo gallery viewers where auto-advance with pause is standard.

detector.signalDescription: Detect carousel implementations with auto-advance configuration (setInterval on slide transition, autoPlay/autoplay options in carousel libraries). Check for associated pause control elements.
limitations: CSS-only animation carousels may auto-advance without JavaScript signals.
Cannot determine if a pause button is added dynamically at runtime.
Some carousel libraries use different configuration keys for auto-advance.

scenarios: Kiosk displays with no user input where auto-advance is appropriate.
User-initiated slideshows where the user presses play.
mitigations: Check for pause button or hover-pause behavior alongside auto-advance detection.

fixturePlan.description: Hero section with 3 slides and setInterval(4000) advancing slides, no pause button.
Same hero section with manual arrows and dots only, no auto-advance.
Carousel with auto-advance at 6-second intervals with visible pause button and hover-pause.
